dimension_notes.naming_quality: Names are generally descriptive. Some internal helpers like _run_dense, _run_sparse, _build_result in _core.py are generic. Module-level names (als.py, bpr.py) are domain-appropriate.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Core mining dispatch in _core.py has clear branching logic. Re-import of time/numpy inside verbose blocks is confusing. The is_empty pattern in model.py is duplicated across methods.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Good use of TYPE_CHECKING imports and typing.cast. _rusket.pyi stubs exist. Some Any escape hatches (kwargs storage, _convert_to_orig_type). Overloaded signatures in transactions.py are well done.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: BaseModel.from_transactions is abstract and consistently implemented. fit()/mine()/predict() contracts are clear. Some implicit contracts (e.g. self._result must be set by fit).
dimension_notes.error_consistency: RuntimeError for unfitted models, ValueError for bad inputs, TypeError for wrong types — consistent pattern. Some bare except/pass blocks in model.py (e.g. line 71-72).
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: RuleMinerMixin, BaseModel, Miner, ImplicitRecommender hierarchy is well-designed. EmbeddingMixin for factor-based models is appropriate. Some methods are too large (mine_grouped ~100 lines with 3 paths).
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: Repeated import_optional_dependency calls (sometimes 2-3 consecutive imports of the same pattern). Duplicate is_empty checks across methods. Verbose timing blocks are copy-pasted.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Clear separation: Rust core (src/) handles computation, Python (rusket/) handles API surface. Module per algorithm is clean. __init__.py exports are comprehensive.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: from_transactions → to_dataframe → _from_dataframe pipeline is clear. Some modules have tight coupling (model.py imports from association_rules, spark, mine). Type detection via string comparison is fragile.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Verbose timing blocks use repeated import time pattern (already imported at module level). col_names computed twice in dispatch() (line 223 and 229). _convert_to_orig_type has tuple listed twice in isinstance check.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: Clean layering: _rusket (Rust FFI) → _core (dispatch) → algorithm modules → model base classes. Some circular-ish patterns with import_optional_dependency calls.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: _auto_detect_cuda() runs at import time in __init__.py — side effect on import. Lazy imports via __getattr__ partially mitigate heavy optional deps.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: Consistent NumPy-style docstrings. verbose uses int (not bool) as documented. seed not random_state. Some files use from rusket._dependencies import_optional_dependency redundantly.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Core deps (numpy, scipy) are reasonable. Optional deps (polars, pyspark, pyarrow, faiss, tqdm) are properly gated. No version conflicts apparent.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: 76 test files covering most algorithms. Benchmark tests mixed with unit tests. Some tests are large integration tests. conftest.py provides shared fixtures.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: Consistent from_transactions/from_pandas/from_polars/from_spark pattern. mine()/fit()/predict() sklearn-compatible API. recommend_items deprecated properly.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Not applicable — this is a library, not an application. No auth patterns needed. Score reflects absence of concern rather than quality.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: recommend_items deprecated but still present. from_pandas/from_polars are thin wrappers over from_transactions. SparseDataFrame deprecation warning still checked.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Flat structure with 60 files in rusket/. No sub-packages for grouping (e.g. miners/, recommenders/). scripts/ and docs/ are logically separated.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Consistent patterns: all algorithms follow Miner or ImplicitRecommender base. _convert_to_orig_type pattern for type preservation is consistent but verbose.

findings.summary: Repeated consecutive import_optional_dependency calls in multiple files
evidence: _core.py lines 107-112: import_optional_dependency called twice consecutively for pd and pa
model.py lines 621-626: consecutive import of _pd and _pl with duplicate from rusket._dependencies import
transactions.py lines 136-144: three consecutive import_optional_dependency calls
findings.suggestion: Consolidate consecutive import_optional_dependency calls into single blocks. Consider a helper that imports multiple optional deps at once.

findings.summary: Identical is_empty check pattern duplicated across methods in model.py
evidence: Lines 57-72 and lines 177-189 contain identical is_empty checking logic testing for None, .empty, .is_empty(), .isEmpty(), and len()
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared _is_dataframe_empty(df) utility function to eliminate the duplicated pattern.

findings.summary: Module-level time import shadowed by repeated conditional imports in _core.py
evidence: time is imported at line 4 (module level) but also re-imported inside verbose blocks at lines 47, 53, 60, 78, 87, 94, 171
findings.suggestion: Remove the redundant 'import time' statements inside verbose blocks since time is already imported at module level.

findings.summary: col_names computed twice in dispatch() for CSR path
evidence: Line 223: col_names = column_names or [str(i) for i in range(n_cols)] and Line 229: col_names = column_names or [str(i) for i in range(n_cols)] — exact duplicate line
findings.suggestion: Remove the duplicate col_names assignment at line 229.

findings.summary: tuple listed twice in isinstance checks in _convert_to_orig_type
evidence: Line 538: isinstance(x, (tuple, set, tuple)) — tuple appears twice in the type tuple
findings.suggestion: Fix isinstance checks to use (tuple, set) without the duplicate tuple entry.

findings.summary: _auto_detect_cuda() executes at import time causing side effects
evidence: __init__.py line 72: _auto_detect_cuda() is called at module import time, probing for CUDA hardware on every import of rusket
findings.suggestion: Consider deferring CUDA detection to first use or making it opt-in, to avoid import-time side effects and reduce import latency for non-CUDA users.

findings.summary: DataFrame type detection relies on string comparison of class names
findings.suggestion: Consider centralizing type detection into a helper function or enum, rather than repeating string-based type checks across modules.

findings.summary: Some files import from rusket._dependencies with full path, others use relative imports
evidence: Mixing absolute and relative imports within the same file
findings.suggestion: Standardize on relative imports throughout the rusket package for internal references.

findings.summary: 60 files in a flat package structure without sub-packages
evidence: All 60 Python files are in the rusket/ root: miners (fpgrowth.py, eclat.py, fin.py, lcm.py, negfin.py), recommenders (als.py, bpr.py, ease.py, item_knn.py, svd.py, lightgcn.py, sasrec.py, bert4rec.py), utilities (transactions.py, evaluation.py, splitting.py), etc.
findings.suggestion: Consider grouping related modules into sub-packages (e.g. rusket/miners/, rusket/recommenders/, rusket/utils/) for better navigability as the library grows.

findings.summary: RuleMinerMixin.recommend_items() is deprecated but still in the public API
evidence: Lines 222-231: recommend_items warns about deprecation but is still present and importable
findings.suggestion: Plan removal timeline. Consider adding removal version to the deprecation warning message.

findings.summary: Benchmark tests are colocated with unit tests in the tests/ directory
evidence: test_benchmark.py (8574 bytes), test_benchmark_pca.py (4977 bytes), test_benchmark_pipeline.py (5486 bytes) are in tests/ alongside unit tests
findings.suggestion: Tag benchmark tests with pytest markers or move them to a benchmarks/ subdirectory so they can be easily excluded from CI unit test runs.

findings.summary: Bare except/pass block swallows all errors silently
evidence: Lines 69-72: except Exception: pass — silently swallows any error when trying to check if df_freq is empty via len()
findings.suggestion: Log or handle the exception explicitly, or at minimum catch a more specific exception type.
